questions.ex: Pakistan withdrew from CENTO in 1979.

questions.q: When was the Objective Resolution passed?
questions.ex: The Objective Resolution was passed in 1949.

questions.q: When was the first constitution of Pakistan enforced?
questions.ex: Pakistan's first constitution was enforced in 1956.

questions.q: What is the capital of Turkey?
questions.ex: The capital of Turkey is Ankara.

questions.q: Which is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.ex: The Pacific Ocean is the largest.

subcategories.title: FIA Assistant Sub Inspector (ASI) Guess Paper - 100 MCQs

questions.ex: Abstain means to refrain from doing something.
Fragile means easily broken; robust means strong and healthy.
'Unique' starts with a consonant 'y' sound, so 'a' is used.
The correct spelling is 'Accommodation' (double c, double m).

questions.q: Change to Passive Voice: 'He opens the door.'
options: The door is opened by him.
The door was opened by him.
The door is open by him.
The door has opened by him.
questions.ex: Simple present 'opens' becomes 'is opened' in passive voice.

questions.q: Choose the correct preposition: 'He is fond ___ music.'
questions.ex: 'Fond' is always followed by the preposition 'of'.

questions.q: A person who hates mankind is called:
questions.ex: A misanthrope is someone who dislikes or avoids human society.

questions.q: Meaning of the idiom 'To cry wolf':
questions.ex: To cry wolf means to raise a false alarm.

questions.q: Identify the part of speech: 'The cat sat SILENTLY.'
questions.ex: 'Silently' describes how the cat sat, making it an adverb.

questions.q: Complete the sentence: 'If it rains, I ___ stay home.'
questions.ex: This is a first conditional sentence (If + present, will + base verb).
Enormous means very large or huge.
Vague means unclear; clear is its opposite.

questions.q: She is '___' best student in class.
questions.ex: Superlative degrees ('best') take the definite article 'the'.

questions.q: One who can neither read nor write is:
questions.ex: Illiterate is the term for someone who cannot read or write.
'Break the ice' means to initiate social interaction.
'Afraid' is followed by the preposition 'of'.
Precise means exact or accurate.
Ancient means very old; modern is the opposite.
Present perfect continuous ('have been') is used for actions starting in the past and continuing.
The irregular plural of mouse is mice.

questions.q: The headquarter of Interpol is in:
questions.ex: Interpol is headquartered in Lyon, France.

questions.q: Which is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.ex: Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world.
The Turkish Lira is the official currency of Turkey.

questions.q: Which planet is known as the 'Blue Planet'?
questions.ex: Earth is called the Blue Planet due to its abundant water.

questions.q: Who is the current Secretary-General of the UN?
questions.ex: António Guterres is the current UN Secretary-General.

questions.q: The 'Great Wall' is located in which country?
questions.ex: The Great Wall of China is a famous landmark in China.

questions.q: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.ex: The Nile is generally considered the longest river in the world.

questions.q: The Nobel Prize is awarded in how many categories?
questions.ex: Nobel Prizes are awarded in Physics, Chemistry, Physiology/Medicine, Literature, Peace, and Economics (6 categories).

questions.q: The largest producer of oil in the world is:
questions.ex: As of recent years, the USA has become the top oil producer.

questions.q: Mount Everest is located in which country?
questions.ex: Mount Everest is located in the Himalayas on the border of Nepal and China, with the summit in Nepal.

questions.q: The deepest ocean in the world is:
questions.ex: The Pacific Ocean is the deepest and largest ocean.

questions.q: The 'Statue of Liberty' was a gift from which country to the USA?
questions.ex: France gifted the Statue of Liberty to the USA.
Moscow is the capital of Russia.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of Geometry'?
questions.ex: Euclid is often referred to as the Father of Geometry.

questions.q: The largest continent by population is:
questions.ex: Asia is the most populous continent.
Henry Dunant founded the International Committee of the Red Cross.

questions.q: Which country has the largest parliament in the world?
questions.ex: The National People's Congress of China is the largest parliamentary body.
Canberra is the capital city of Australia.
The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France.

questions.q: Which gas is most abundant in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.ex: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere.

questions.q: What is the shortcut key to 'Undo' in Windows?
questions.ex: Ctrl+Z is the shortcut for Undo.

questions.q: Which of the following is an Operating System?
questions.ex: Linux is a famous open-source operating system.
1 GB = 1024 Megabytes.
The CPU performs all processing, hence called the brain.
HTTP is the foundation of data communication for the WWW.

questions.q: Which key is used to refresh a webpage?
questions.ex: F5 is the standard key to refresh or reload a page.

questions.q: Which of the following is 'Permanent' storage?
questions.ex: ROM (Read-Only Memory) is non-volatile and permanent.

questions.q: What is the extension of an MS Word file (modern)?
questions.ex: .docx is the default extension for MS Word documents.

questions.q: A computer on a network that requests files from another computer is a:
questions.ex: The client requests services, and the server provides them.
Microsoft developed the Windows operating system.

questions.q: The binary system uses only two digits:
questions.ex: Binary uses 0 (off) and 1 (on).
PDF stands for Portable Document Format.

questions.q: Which device is used for connecting a computer to the internet via phone line?
questions.ex: A modem modulates/demodulates signals for internet access.

questions.q: The intersection of a row and a column in Excel is called a:
questions.ex: A cell is the basic unit of an Excel worksheet.

questions.q: Which of the following is an 'Output' device?
questions.ex: A monitor displays output to the user.

questions.q: What is the shortcut key for 'Copy'?
questions.ex: Ctrl+C is used to copy selected items.

questions.q: A 'Byte' consists of how many bits?
questions.ex: 1 Byte = 8 Bits.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of Computers'?
questions.ex: Charles Babbage designed the first mechanical computer.

questions.q: Which software is used for browsing the internet?
questions.ex: Google Chrome is a popular web browser.
GUI allows users to interact with devices through visual icons.

questions.q: How many years did the Holy Quran take for complete revelation?
questions.ex: The Quran was revealed over a period of approximately 23 years.

questions.q: The 'Battle of Badr' took place in which Hijri year?
questions.ex: The Battle of Badr occurred in 2 A.H.

questions.q: Which Prophet is known as 'Abul Bashar'?
questions.ex: Hazrat Adam (AS) is called Abul Bashar (Father of Mankind).

questions.q: How many 'Sajdahs' are there in the Holy Quran?
questions.ex: There are 14 mandatory Sajdah-e-Tilawat in the Quran.

questions.ex: Hazrat Bilal Habshi (RA) was the first Muazzin.

questions.q: Which Surah is called the 'Heart of Quran'?
questions.ex: Surah Yaseen is famously known as the Heart of the Quran.

questions.q: The 'Treaty of Hudaibiya' was signed in:
questions.ex: The treaty was signed in 6 A.H.

questions.q: The largest Surah of the Holy Quran is:
questions.ex: Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest Surah.

questions.q: How many daughters did the Holy Prophet (SAW) have?
questions.ex: The Holy Prophet (SAW) had four daughters: Zainab, Ruqayyah, Umm Kulthum, and Fatima (RA).

questions.q: Which is the highest peak of Pakistan?
questions.ex: K2 (8611m) is the highest in Pakistan and 2nd in the world.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Liaquat Ali Khan was the first PM.

questions.q: The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan was enforced on:
questions.ex: It was enforced on 14 August 1973.

questions.q: The largest province of Pakistan by area is:
questions.ex: Balochistan covers about 44% of Pakistan's land area.

questions.q: Which city is known as the 'City of Lights'?
questions.ex: Karachi is famously called the City of Lights.
It was signed in 1916 between Muslim League and Congress.

questions.q: Who wrote the national anthem of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Hafeez Jalandhari wrote the lyrics of the anthem.
It was signed after the 1965 war in January 1966.

questions.q: Which is the national flower of Pakistan?
questions.ex: Jasmine (Chambeli) is the national flower.

questions.q: If 'RED' is coded as 360, what is the code for 'CAT'?
questions.ex: Pattern: Position in alphabet multiplied (C=3, A=1, T=20 -> 3*1*20 = 60).
All are prime numbers except 9 (which is divisible by 3).
These are squares of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Next is 6^2 = 36.

questions.q: A clock shows 3:00. What is the angle between the hands?
questions.ex: At 3:00, the hour hand is at 3 and minute hand at 12, forming 90 degrees.

questions.q: If 10 men can do a work in 5 days, how many days will 5 men take?
questions.ex: Inverse proportion: 10*5 = 5*x -> x = 10.
Subtracting 10 each time.
The word is reversed.

questions.q: A son's age is 1/3 of his father's. If father is 45, how old is the son?
The FIA Act 1974 consists of how many sections?
questions.ex: The original Act has 10 sections.

questions.q: Who has the power to amend the Schedule of the FIA Act?
questions.ex: The Federal Government can amend the Schedule by notification in the official Gazette.

questions.q: The Director General of FIA is appointed by:
questions.ex: Section 3(2) states the DG is appointed by the Federal Government.

questions.q: Under Section 5, an FIA officer has the power of an 'Officer-in-Charge' of a:
questions.ex: Section 5(1) gives powers of an officer-in-charge of a police station.

questions.q: FIA was established to replace which organization?
questions.ex: FIA replaced the Pakistan Special Police Establishment (PSPE).

questions.q: The 'Superintendence' of the Agency vests in the:
questions.ex: Section 4(1) states superintendence vests in the Federal Government.

questions.q: Which section of the FIA Act deals with 'Indemnity' (protection of action taken in good faith)?
questions.ex: Section 8 provides indemnity to the Federal Government and Agency members.

questions.q: The FIA can investigate offenses committed on:
questions.ex: The Act extends to the whole of Pakistan and also applies to citizens/public servants wherever they may be.

questions.q: Which section deals with the 'Power to make rules'?
questions.ex: Section 9 empowers the Federal Government to make rules.

questions.q: The 'Administration' of the Agency vests in the:
questions.ex: Section 4(2) states the administration vests in the Director General.
The correct spelling is 'Committee' (double m, double t, double e).
Fast and quick are synonyms.
Ugly is the opposite of beautiful.
'a' is used before consonant sounds.

questions.q: Identify the noun: 'Ali is a good boy.'
questions.ex: 'Ali' is a proper noun.

questions.q: Choose the correct preposition: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.ex: We use 'on' for surfaces.
Nouns ending in 'x' take 'es' for plural.
Night is the opposite of day.
Third-person singular takes 's' in present tense.
It means someone very precious or dear.
Glad means happy.
Hot is the opposite of cold.
'I' takes the base form of the verb.
The correct spelling is 'Grammar'.

questions.q: A person who treats patients is a:
questions.ex: A doctor treats patients.
Universal truths are in simple present tense.
Large and big have the same meaning.
Poor is the opposite of rich.
'an' is used before vowel sounds.
'runs' is an action word (verb).
